title: Arrive for an Interview on Time
summary: Plan your route and timing so you arrive at an interview calm, prepared, and a few minutes early.
learnerPromise: You will be able to plan your travel, gather your materials, and arrive at an interview location with enough time to feel calm and collected.
whyItMatters: Being late creates stress and a poor first impression. Arriving early gives you time to settle in and shows the employer you are reliable and respectful.
realLifeUses: Getting to an in-person interview at a store or office you have never visited before
Arriving for a group interview or job fair with multiple employers
Planning around public transit schedules or traffic to be on time
Knowing what to bring and how to present yourself when you check in
materialsNeeded: Directions or map to the interview location
A phone with the contact number saved
result: A completed travel plan and a checklist of materials, with you arriving 10 to 15 minutes before the interview time.
youWillLearn: How to plan your route and check travel times in advance
How to build in buffer time for delays like traffic or transit issues
What to bring and how to organize your materials
How to check in professionally and wait calmly
miniChallenge: Plan the route to a real or imaginary interview location. Check the travel time, decide when to leave, and pack a small bag with everything you would bring.
steps: Look up the interview address and plan your route the day before. Check traffic or transit schedules for the time of day you will be traveling.
Aim to arrive 10 to 15 minutes early. Set a goal to leave your home with at least 15 minutes of extra buffer time built in.
Pack a folder or bag with two printed copies of your resume, a notepad and pen, your ID, and the interviewer's phone number.
Dress in your prepared outfit and do a final check in the mirror. Make sure you are clean, neat, and comfortable.
When you arrive, tell the receptionist or first person you see your name, who you are meeting, and the time of your interview. Turn your phone to silent.
completionCriteria: You have a written route plan and know exactly when you need to leave to arrive 10 to 15 minutes early
You have a packed bag or folder with your resume copies, a pen, and the interviewer's contact number
commonProblems: Underestimating travel time — check the route at the same time of day as your interview, not at midnight
Forgetting to save the interviewer's phone number in case you are delayed
Arriving too early — more than 20 minutes can be awkward; wait in a nearby coffee shop if needed
Rushing and forgetting materials — pack your bag the night before
tips: Do a practice trip to the location a day or two before if it is nearby and unfamiliar
Set more than one alarm if the interview is early in the morning
Bring cash or a transit card in case of payment issues on the way
If you are running late despite your best effort, call the interviewer as soon as you know, explain briefly, and give a realistic arrival time
